evals.prompt: Create a new automation script called 'invoice-importer'. It will read CSV invoices from the input directory and write a summary JSON to the output directory.
evals.expected_output: Scaffolded scripts/invoice-importer/ with package.json, tsconfig.json, and src/main.ts using M3LScript. Root tsconfig.json updated with the new project reference. pnpm install run to link the workspace.
expectations: Creates scripts/invoice-importer/package.json with name '@m3l-automation/invoice-importer', type 'module', and workspace:* dependency on m3l-common
Creates scripts/invoice-importer/tsconfig.json extending tsconfig.base.json with correct rootDir/outDir and a project reference to m3l-common
Creates scripts/invoice-importer/src/main.ts that imports Core from @m3l-automation/m3l-common and uses M3LScript lifecycle
Adds the new package reference to the root tsconfig.json references array
Runs pnpm install to link the workspace
Does not hardcode data/, input/, or output/ paths (uses M3LPaths instead)

evals.prompt: Build a script named 'example-automation' — it should fetch reports from an API.
evals.expected_output: Skill detects that scripts/example-automation/ already exists and refuses to overwrite it, telling the user to pick a different name.
expectations: Skill checks whether scripts/example-automation/ already exists
Skill refuses to scaffold and tells the user the directory already exists
No files are created or overwritten

evals.prompt: Add a job that polls an S3 bucket for new files every 5 minutes and archives them locally.
evals.expected_output: Skill asks for a name if not provided (or derives one), then scaffolds the script with M3LScript. The src/main.ts uses Core.M3LScript and M3LPaths for path resolution, not hardcoded paths.
expectations: Skill either asks for a script name or derives a kebab-case name from the description
Generated src/main.ts uses Core.M3LScript lifecycle (construct + run)
No CommonJS constructs (require, module.exports, __dirname) in any generated file
All relative imports in generated TypeScript carry the .js extension
